plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-298 RP08.P09.M06.S01-RP08.P09.M10.S10 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-298 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP08.P09 review phase and the entire RP08 program descriptor scope (M06 fixture review cycle, M07 test/golden cycle with review receipt placeholder and future correction slot, M08 Hermes packet cycle, M09 Claude review packet cycle, M10 closeout head) across five sections over 92 planned rows with a pack-local row-extras constant, Hermes evidence (H08), Claude review packet (C08), and a CP00-299 handoff routing to the RP09 program bootstrap, without opening permission bypass, UI leaks, command runtime execution, Hermes runtime receipts, state writes, object storage, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-298 is a descriptor-only closeout pack that closes the RP08.P09 review phase and the entire RP08 program descriptor scope, handing off to CP00-299 / RP09.P00.M00.S01. Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claim ("implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout", production_ready=false) is supported by source, tests, contract/validator pins, and boundary attestations. (1) The CP298 pack binding (registry.js:469) matches the baseline exactly — 92 units, risk C, range RP08.P09.M06.S01-RP08.P09.M10.S10, upstream CP00-297, next CP00-299 with next_subphase RP09.P00.M00.S01 — and is deep-equaled by the contract current_pack (contract:16-31) and the contract validator script (validate-rp08-email-dms-core-contract.mjs:357). (2) Coverage math is exact: micro-phase counts 20/22/20/20/10 and deliverable counts (claude_review 14, hermes_evidence 1, implementation 57, security_audit 10, test 5, ui 5) both total 92 and match the baseline distributions; validateEmailDmsCoreCp298Coverage (validators.js:6020) enforces pack_id/risk/unit_count/first+last unit/no-duplicate/RP08-only plus every distribution and per-section row title. (3) All 7 required public exports exist and are wired through index.js, exercised by three dedicated tests (model.test.js:1696-1745) asserting coverage.valid, descriptor validity, descriptor-only rows, and the RP08->RP09 handoff. (4) No-write/no-real-data/no-runtime boundaries are thoroughly attested in freezeCp298Result (service.js:3675), the CP298 no-write attestation, and the manifest boundary block (manifest:3643-3677). (5) Authority boundary is correct: Claude is not final approver, human final approval required, and enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation (descriptor authority_boundary; manifest:3664-3665; validators.js:6108-6115). (6) CP178+ review-evidence hardening is encoded in forbidden_review_evidence (registry.js:652), rejecting auth failures, zero-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled-tool runs, and missing references. (7) Hermes control-plane patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packets/receipts (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). The untracked docs/ldip-integration/claude-review-results/c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json belongs to a separate LDIP workstream and is correctly classified as unrelated; the injection vector it reported in new-session-handoff.md is not present in the current file. One cosmetic, non-blocking classification note is recorded below. No P0/P1/P2 issues; pack and goal closeout are not blocked. Per the authority boundary, this read-only review is independent input only and is not final approval — human final approval remains required.

findings.title: changed-file-scope lists the pack's own cp00-298 artifact directory under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 100-105) includes docs/closeout-packs/cp00-298/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md, but docs/closeout-packs/cp00-298/ is this pack's own manifest/command-evidence directory and is simultaneously (and correctly) listed under untracked_implementation_files (line 97). The label 'unrelated' is therefore semantically imprecise for a pack-owned artifact directory. This is cosmetic only: the authoritative untracked_implementation_files field classifies it correctly, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, and the preservation list's actual purpose (record untracked files that were preserved rather than cleaned) is still satisfied. No closeout gate depends on this label.
findings.recommendation: Optionally tighten the scope generator so pack-owned untracked artifacts (the docs/closeout-packs/<pack>/ directory) are not also enumerated in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, to avoid future readers/automation inferring the pack's own evidence is unrelated. Non-blocking; no action required for CP00-298 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: informational_non_blocking; acknowledged as cosmetic classification overlap; does not block pack or goal closeout and requires no remediation before commit

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-298 email dms core p09 closeout descriptor pack
